element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn rotation and line-clearing mechanics with no time pressure initially
Fundamental feedback loop; completed lines disappear and blocks fall, teaching spatial reasoning
Difficulty ramps as blocks fall faster; player must adapt timing and planning
Static puzzle state; player must plan color-matching strategy without falling-block pressure
Adds tactical depth; pills can be rotated to match either color, increasing puzzle complexity
Players must adapt between two distinct mechanics under time pressure; context-switching challenge

typical_relief_triggers: Completed line in Tetris (visual/audio feedback, blocks disappear)
Virus cleared in Dr. Mario (stage progression toward completion)
Mode transition in Mixed Match (brief mental reset between game types)
typical_tension_triggers: Stack height approaching top of screen (Tetris)
Pills approaching top of screen (Dr. Mario)
Time pressure in Mixed Match mode
Mode transition requiring mechanic switch (Mixed Match)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Dual-paced: Tetris features continuous escalating pressure through gravity and block accumulation; Dr. Mario offers puzzle-solving pause with strategic planning time. Mixed Match alternates tension/relief by switching modes, preventing exhaustion while maintaining engagement.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tetris A-Type and B-Type modes available at start; players can self-select difficulty. Dr. Mario offers customizable difficulty before gameplay begins.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Gravity acceleration in Tetris creates escalating pressure. Dr. Mario complexity increases with virus count and board density.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Stack-height tension in Tetris becomes critical; one poor placement ends run. Dr. Mario requires precise multi-step virus elimination plans.
notable_spikes: Tetris: Gravity increase thresholds at defined score/line milestones
Dr. Mario: Difficulty levels (Low/Medium/High) create discrete jumps in virus density
Mixed Match: Mode transitions create cognitive friction; players must shift mental models mid-session

notable_level_patterns.description: Viruses arranged in balanced patterns requiring systematic bottom-up clearing to cascade and eliminate upper viruses
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Dr. Mario stages (exact stage numbers not documented)

notable_level_patterns.description: As lines accumulate without clears, stack approaches top; single mistake becomes fatal; requires forward planning of 2-3 pieces
Rapid transitions between Tetris (real-time pressure) and Dr. Mario (puzzle planning) force context-switching under time limits

sources_used: wikipedia: Tetris & Dr. Mario gameplay section
